claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 19 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 19 and in approved education
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 19 and in approved education; Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is 19 and in approved education
Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children you're responsible for, and if so, how many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I have one child - Alex. He's 19 and lives with me at home. I'd like to know if I can claim Child Benefit for him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I can see you have one child, Alex, who is 19 years old and lives with you.

Since Alex is 19, a key factor for eligibility is whether he is in approved education or training. Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 if they're in approved education or training.

Is Alex currently in approved education or training (such as A-levels, higher education, or an approved training course)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he is! He's studying for his A-levels at the local sixth form college.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great! Since Alex is 19, living with you, and studying for his A-levels at sixth form college, this counts as approved education.

Just to confirm a couple more details:

Does Alex work for 24 hours or more per week, or does he receive any benefits in his own right (such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, he doesn't work that many hours - just a few hours on the weekend to have some pocket money. And he doesn't receive any benefits in his own right either.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent, thank you for confirming that.

One final question: Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news!** You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Here's why:
- Alex is 19 years old and studying for his A-levels at sixth form college, which counts as approved education
- Child Benefit can continue for children under 20 who are in approved education or training
- Alex lives with you in the UK
- He only works a few hours on weekends (not 24+ hours per week)
- He doesn't receive any benefits in his own right

You meet all the eligibility requirements for Child Benefit for Alex.

**Important note:** If you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge. However, this doesn't affect your eligibility - you can still claim Child Benefit.

If you haven't already claimed, you can apply for Child Benefit. If Alex's circumstances change (for example, if he finishes his A-levels, starts work for 24+ hours per week, or starts receiving benefits), you should inform HMRC as this could affect your entitlement.

reasonings: Alex is 19 years old and studying for his A-levels at sixth form college, which qualifies as approved education. Under the Child Benefit rules, you can continue to receive Child Benefit for a child under 20 if they stay in approved education or training. Alex lives with the claimant in the UK, does not work 24 hours or more per week (only a few hours on weekends), and does not receive any benefits in his own right such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit. The claimant is responsible for Alex and lives in the UK. Therefore, all eligibility criteria are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for one child, Alex, aged 19. Alex is in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form college), lives at home with the claimant, works only a few hours per week (not 24+ hours), and receives no benefits in his own right. All eligibility requirements for Child Benefit are satisfied.

Alex lives with the claimant in the UK, does not work 24 hours or more per week (only a few hours on weekends), and does not receive any benefits in his own right such as Employment and Support Allowance or Universal Credit. The claimant is responsible for Alex and lives in the UK. Therefore, all eligibility criteria are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant lives in the UK (Manchester) and is responsible for one child, Alex, aged 19. Alex is in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form college), lives at home with the claimant, works only a few hours per week (not 24+ hours), and receives no benefits in his own right. All eligibility requirements for Child Benefit are satisfied.
